concepts.label: Cryptocurrency
concepts.reason: The header introduces the central topic of the document as a domain-specific financial technology term.

paragraphs.text: Bitcoin was created in 2008 by an anonymous person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto. The whitepaper titled "Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System" introduced a decentralized digital currency that operates without central banks or intermediaries.

concepts.label: Bitcoin as a decentralized digital currency without intermediaries
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin decentralized digital currency without central banks or intermediaries
concepts.reason: This sentence gives an explicit characterization of what Bitcoin is and how it operates.

concepts.reason: This is a specific cited work that can be directly verified and grounded.

concepts.label: Blockchain technology
concepts.reason: The header names the central topic/domain of the section.

paragraphs.text: A blockchain is a distributed ledger that records all transactions across a network of computers. Each block contains a cryptographic hash of the previous block, creating an immutable chain. Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second, while Visa handles around 24,000.

concepts.label: Bitcoin blockchain transaction throughput
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin blockchain transactions per second roughly 7
concepts.reason: The paragraph gives a specific throughput figure for Bitcoin that can be checked against technical sources.
surfaceForms: Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second

concepts.reason: This is a verifiable technical claim about how blocks are linked and why the chain is considered immutable.

concepts.reason: The paragraph provides a quantitative comparison figure for Visa that benefits from verification.

paragraphs.text: The consensus mechanism Bitcoin uses is called Proof of Work, where miners compete to solve complex mathematical puzzles. The first miner to solve the puzzle adds the next block and receives newly minted bitcoin as a reward. This reward started at 50 BTC and halves approximately every four years.

concepts.label: Bitcoin block reward halving schedule
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin block reward started at 50 BTC and halves approximately every four years
concepts.reason: Specific historical and periodic reward claim with quantitative details.

concepts.label: Block reward for the winning miner
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin first miner to solve puzzle adds the next block and receives newly minted bitcoin as a reward
concepts.reason: Verifiable statement about how block creation and rewards are assigned in Bitcoin.

concepts.reason: Core consensus method explicitly named and briefly described.

paragraphs.text: Ethereum was proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013 and launched in 2015. It introduced smart contracts—self-executing programs stored on the blockchain that automatically enforce agreements when conditions are met.

concepts.label: Ethereum proposal and launch timeline

concepts.reason: Verifiable historical claim with attribution and dates.

paragraphs.text: In September 2022, Ethereum transitioned from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake in an event called "The Merge." This reduced Ethereum's energy consumption by approximately 99.95% according to the Ethereum Foundation.

concepts.label: Ethereum's energy consumption dropped by approximately 99.95% after The Merge

concepts.reason: This is a quantitative reported outcome attributed to a source and should be verified.

concepts.reason: This is a dated factual claim about Ethereum's consensus change that should be verified.

concepts.reason: This is the named organization cited as the source of the energy reduction figure.

concepts.label: Decentralized finance
concepts.reason: The header introduces the central domain topic of the section.

paragraphs.text: DeFi applications allow users to lend, borrow, and trade without traditional financial intermediaries. The total value locked in DeFi protocols peaked at over $180 billion in late 2021 before declining significantly.

paragraphs.text: Uniswap, launched in 2018, pioneered the automated market maker model where liquidity providers deposit token pairs into pools. The protocol has processed over $1.8 trillion in trading volume since its launch.

paragraphs.text: The SEC has classified most cryptocurrencies as securities, with notable exceptions for Bitcoin and Ethereum. The landmark SEC v. Ripple case in 2023 established that programmatic sales of tokens on exchanges do not necessarily constitute securities transactions.

concepts.label: SEC classification of most cryptocurrencies as securities, with Bitcoin and Ethereum as exceptions
concepts.searchQuery: SEC classified most cryptocurrencies as securities exceptions Bitcoin Ethereum
concepts.reason: This is a broad regulatory claim with specific exceptions that should be verified against SEC statements and legal guidance.

concepts.reason: This is the specific legal outcome attributed to the Ripple case and is directly verifiable from the ruling.
